mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 52% over any 1-year rolling window, or forward 5-day returns in triggered periods do not significantly underperform baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts combined with persistent inflation compresses real consumer demand and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend threshold, it indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high production periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 52% over any 1-year rolling window, or forward 60-day returns in growth periods do not significantly outperform baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of elevated policy rates and high inflation erodes real disposable income and corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint threshold signal fails to predict negative returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly employment gains signal resilient consumer spending power and economic momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high employment periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales revenue coupled with stable housing construction indicates broad-based consumer demand resilience, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across three consecutive rolling 3-year windows.
